scope: Safety, evidence and resolution rules controlling automatic locksmith workflow recommendations

default_policy.reason: A workflow is only recommended automatically when the vehicle application, platform, job context, key state and tool route are sufficiently resolved.

conditions: The workshop event matches the resolved platform and job type
The linked workflow and procedure steps exist
The evidence applies to the exact market, platform and operation
Tool support and required accessories are separately resolved
The procedure is not contradicted by a newer security generation
Require user confirmation before exposing procedural detail
Do not promote the route above a workshop-verified alternative
Do not infer a procedure from model and year alone

hard_blocks.when: platform_id is missing or multiple incompatible platform candidates remain
job type is not resolved as add_key, all_keys_lost, replacement, clone, renew, reset or another defined operation
working-key availability changes the acquisition or programming route and has not been answered
a workflow belongs to a different security generation, such as FBS3 versus FBS4, MQB48 versus MQB5C or first-generation BDC versus BDC2/BDC3
a shared family name is being used to transfer a procedure between marques without exact application evidence
a tool profile lists a general capability but no exact workflow or application support is linked
remote or proximity key frequency and application are unresolved
a used immobiliser, BCM, BSI, KVM, ESL or similar module is proposed but virgin, personalised, locked or donor state is unknown

recommendation_output_contract.reasoning_requirement: Every recommendation must explain why the route was selected and why excluded alternatives were rejected.
recommendation_output_contract.warning_requirement: Blocking warnings must appear before procedural steps.
